classification_rationale: The donateToReserves() function in Euler's eToken contract has a validation-gap: it burns eTokens (collateral) but not dTokens (debt), allowing the position to become insolvent without a health check. Attacker uses this to artificially create bad debt, then self-liquidates via Euler's dynamic liquidation penalty mechanism to extract the discounted collateral. The lifecycle-hook of the donateToReserves→liquidation sequence is exploitable because the missing health check is a mechanism-level vulnerability in the eToken accounting invariant.
